title: elymus russellii
intro: Elymus russellii, also known as the Russian wild rye, is a perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Elymus russellii include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. Elymus russellii is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be used to make a decoction or tincture for its medicinal properties.
